title: 11 Transferring Government of Canada Bonds
body_text:
11	Transferring Government of Canada Bonds
11.1	Overview of Transferring Government of Canada Bonds
Central 1 handles transfers of bonds issued by the Government of Canada. Credit unions must submit requests for transfers of sundry marketable bonds directly to the agent specified on the bond.
Canada Savings Bonds are non-assignable and non-transferable except in the event of the Registered Owner芒聙聶s death or legal name change or transfer to an acceptable trust. If bonds have matured, they are not eligible for transfer.
All transfer documents submitted and/or guaranteed by the credit union must be correctly completed. Incorrectly completed documents will be returned to the credit union.
Ensure the bond or proceeds are transferred to the rightful owner or beneficiary. The credit union is liable for errors in change of ownership or payment of proceeds to any other person.
11.2	Transferring Bonds When Registered Owner Changes Name
The Government of Canada only allows bonds to be re-registered if the bondholder is legally changing his or her name. To transfer bonds when there is a legal name change, proceed as follows.
1.
Obtain the original or a certified copy of the document prepared by a Notary Public, a Lawyer, a Court Official, or a Commissioner for Oaths confirming the bondholder芒聙聶s legal name change (e.g., marriage certificate, court order, adoption order). Retain a copy of the document on file at the credit union.
2.
Prepare a Letter of Name Guarantee 芒聙聯 Name Change, Form 1349, signed by a credit union signing officer, confirming that both/all names refer to one and the same person.
Note:	If there are variations in names between the legal document and the name(s) appearing on the bonds/plans, provide a letter of guarantee, stating that the name appearing on the document and the name(s) on the plan/bond(s) refer to one and the same person. For example, 芒聙聹John Henry Smith and John Thomas Henry Smith refer to one and the same person.芒聙聺
3.
Forward the bond(s), the Letter of Name Guarantee 芒聙聯 Name Change, Form 1349, and the original copy of the Notification of Bond Transfer, Form 1324, to Central 1, as per internal credit union transportation policy by courier, at the following address:
Central 1 Credit Union
Payment Services
Attention: Tracing
1441 Creekside Drive
Vancouver, BC V6J 4S7
Related Information
Notification of Bond Transfer,
Form 1324
Letter of Name Guarantee 芒聙聯 Name Change,
Form 1349
11.3	Transferring Bonds When Registered Owner is Deceased
Documentation and procedures for transferring bonds registered to a deceased bondholder will vary according to whether
the deceased died Testate (with a Will) or Intestate (without a Will),
the dollar value of bonds exceeds $75,000 (value depends on the relationship of the beneficiary to the deceased; see
https://www.unclaimedproperties.bankofcanada.ca/
for details),
the bonds are registered with or without rights of survivorship,
the estate is probated, or
a Grant of Administration has been granted.
Note:	In all cases, differences of names must be guaranteed.
Requests for a transfer must be initiated by the properly constituted personal representative(s) of the deceased. The deceased芒聙聶s representative(s) may be required to provide notarial copies of the following documents:
Proof of Death
芒聢聮
Death or Burial certificate issued by Provincial Registrar General/Divisional Registrar/Minister of Religion
芒聢聮
Certificate of Finding of a Coroner
芒聢聮
Certificate issued under the corporate seal of an undertaking establishment/cemetery/crematorium
Note:	Copies of an original document must be certified by a Commissioner for Oaths or a Notary Public with the Notary芒聙聶s signature and seal/stamp of office.
Will (notarial or certified court copy)
Grant of Probate (notarial or certified court copy)
Grant of Administration (notarial or certified court copy)
and
Estate Transfer with Will Form (ETW 2351) or Estate Transfer with No Will Form (ETNW 2351). It is not necessary to have this form notarized if it is
芒聢聮
signed by an authorized credit union signing officer, and
芒聢聮
stamped with the credit union芒聙聶s Endorsement Stamp.
The Estate Transfer with Will Forms (ETW 2351) or the Estate Transfer with No Will Forms (ETNW 2351) are available from the Bank of Canada芒聙聶s Unclaimed Properties Office website at
https://www.unclaimedproperties.bankofcanada.ca/
. The first few pages of each document consist of guidelines for completing the form. See Figure 1 for page 1 of the guidelines of the Estate Transfer with Will Form (ETW 2351). The following pages of the document include Sections A to E of the form. See Figure 2 for page 2 of the form. The Estate Transfer with No Will Form (ETNW 2351) follows a similar format as the Estate Transfer with Will Form (ETW 2351).
Figure 1: Guidelines for Completing the ETW2351 Form, Page 1 of 6
Figure 2: Estate Transfer with Will Form (EW 2351), Page 2 of 6 for Sections A to E
Bonds Registered with Rights of Survivorship
Bonds registered in two or more names 芒聙聹and survivor芒聙聺 can be transferred to the survivor(s). To transfer bonds to the survivor(s), proceed as follows.
1.
Obtain a notarial or original copy of the Proof of Death document. See Chapter 4, Government Bonds.
2.
Obtain a letter from the survivor(s) indicating their Social Insurance Number(s) and complete mailing address(es).
3.
Forward the bonds, Proof of Death document, and letter containing survivor information to Central 1, as per internal credit union transportation policy by courier, at the following address:
Central 1 Credit Union
Payment Services
Attention: Tracing
1441 Creekside Drive
Vancouver, BC V6J 4S7
Bonds Registered Without Rights of Survivorship
Bonds registered in two or more names without 芒聙聹and survivor芒聙聺 specified are
not
automatically transferable to the surviving owner(s). Process the request for transfer in accordance with the type of estate.
If one of the Registered Owners dies Intestate, the remaining Registered Owner is the surviving spouse, and there is no intention to apply for a Grant of Administration, the bond can be transferred to the spouse. See Subsection below, Deceased Died Intestate (No Will) 芒聙聯 Court Order Not Applied For.
Deceased Died Testate (with Will) 芒聙聯 Court Order Not Applied For
If the Registered Owner dies Testate (with a Will) naming the applicant as the beneficiary and the Government of Canada Securities held by the estate do not exceed $75,000 (depending on relationship of the beneficiary to the deceased; see
http://www.csb.gc.ca
for details), and there is no intention of applying for Grant of Probate or Grant of Administration;
OR
if the applicant is the sole beneficiary and would have been entitled to the entire estate even if the deceased had died Intestate and the Government of Canada Securities do not exceed $75,000 (depending on relationship of the beneficiary to the deceased; see
http://www.csb.gc.ca
for details), then proceed as follows.
When the beneficiary first arrives at the credit union
1.
Provide
the
Estate Transfer with Will Form (ETW 2351). Advise the member to complete the form in full. If there is insufficient space on the form to record the bonds, advise the member to attach and initial a separate list.
It is not necessary to have this form notarized if it is
signed by an authorized credit union signing officer and
stamped with the credit union芒聙聶s Endorsement Stamp.
Note:	Any changes or deletions must be stamped 芒聙聹Alterations and Erasure Guaranteed芒聙聺 and signed by both the Declarant and, under the credit union name, a credit union signing officer.
2.
Notify beneficiary(ies) that when the completed Estate Transfer Form (ETRF-2351) is returned to the credit union, it must be accompanied by the following documentation:
A Notarial Copy of the Proof of Death (see Chapter 4, Government Bonds)
A Notarial Copy (with seal) of the Will
All bonds to be transferred
When the beneficiary returns documents to the credit union
1.
Verify the documents as follows:
The Estate Transfer with Will Form (ETW 2351) is completed correctly, signed by an authorized credit union official, stamped with the credit union芒聙聶s Endorsement Stamp, and accompanied by the Proof of Death, Will, and bonds.
The bond numbers listed on the Estate Transfer with Will Form (ETW 2351) are exactly as they appear on the bonds presented.
The Estate Transfer with Will Form (ETW 2351) is dated after any specified survivorship clause contained in the Will.
If there are persons other than the applicant entitled to a portion of the estate, ensure that Sections C and D of the Estate Transfer with Will Form (ETW 2351) have been completed. The submission of an unprobated Will does not reduce the requirements relating to the law of intestacy. If there are no other heirs or next-of-kin, the word 芒聙聹NIL芒聙聺 appears in clause 3b).
Note:	A minor who is entitled to inherit should be listed in Section C, with age and relationship to the deceased specified.
If the name of the deceased varies between the Registered Owner芒聙聶s name on the bond(s) and the name on the Will and/or Proof of Death, the name specified on the Estate Transfer with Will Form (ETW 2351) for the deceased should include name variations as AKA (also known as). If not, a Letter of Name Guarantee 芒聙聯 Estate, Form 1350, from the credit union is required.
2.
If required, complete and guarantee a Letter of Name Guarantee 芒聙聯 Estate, Form 1350, for the deceased and/or the applicant where
芒聢聮
the name of the beneficiary(ies) on the Estate Transfer with Will Form (ETW 2351) is not exactly as stated in the Will and/or to be registered on the transferred bonds, or
芒聢聮
the name of the deceased varies and is not entered on the Estate Transfer Form (ETRF-2351) as AKA.
3.
Complete a Notification of Bond Transfer, Form 1324, in duplicate. Retain copy 2 for credit union files.
4.
Forward the bond(s), Proof of Death, Will, the Estate Transfer with Will Form (ETW 2351), a copy of the Notification of Bond Transfer, Form 1324, and (if required) a Letter of Name Guarantee 芒聙聯 Estate, Form 1350, to Central 1, as per internal credit union transportation policy by courier, at the following address:
Central 1 Credit Union
Payment Services
Attention: Tracing
1441 Creekside Drive
Vancouver, BC V6J 4S7
Related Information
Notification of Bond Transfer,
Form 1324
Letter of Name Guarantee 芒聙聯 Estate,
Form 1350
Deceased Died Testate (with Will) 芒聙聯 Grant of Probate Obtained
If the Registered Owner dies Testate (with a Will) and Government of Canada Securities held by the estate exceed $75,000 (depending on relationship of the beneficiary to the deceased; see
http://www.csb.gc.ca
for details), and Grant of Probate has been granted by the court proceed as follows.
1.
Obtain a notarial or court-certified copy of Grant of Probate.
Note:	If Probate states 芒聙聹Will Attached芒聙聺, a copy of the Will must accompany the Grant of Probate.
2.
Complete and guarantee the Estate Transfer with Will Form (ETW 2351), as instructed in the guidelines on the form.
Note:	Any changes or deletions must be stamped 芒聙聹Alterations and Erasure Guaranteed芒聙聺 and signed by the Declarant and, under the credit union name, a credit union signing officer.
3.
Guarantee the transferor芒聙聶s signature and stamp the Estate Transfer with Will Form (ETW 2351) with the credit union芒聙聶s Endorsement Stamp. Have an authorized signing officer on file with Central 1 sign under the stamp.
4.
If required, complete a Letter of Name Guarantee 芒聙聯 Estate, Form 1350, for the Declarant.
5.
Complete a Notification of Bond Transfer, Form 1324, in duplicate. Retain a copy for the credit union芒聙聶s files.
6.
Forward the bond(s), Grant of Probate, the Estate Transfer with Will Form (ETW 2351), a copy of the Notification of Bond Transfer, and (if necessary) a Letter of Name Guarantee to Central 1, as per internal credit union transportation policy by courier, at the following address:
Central 1 Credit Union
Payment Services
Attention: Tracing
1441 Creekside Drive
Vancouver, BC V6J 4S7
Related Information
Notification of Bond Transfer,
Form 1324
Letter of Name Guarantee 芒聙聯 Estate,
Form 1350
Deceased Died Intestate (No Will) 芒聙聯 Court Order Not Applied For
If the deceased dies Intestate (without a Will) and Government of Canada Securities held by the estate do not exceed $75,000 (depending on relationship of the beneficiary to the deceased; see
https://www.unclaimedproperties.bankofcanada.ca/
for details), and there is no intention to apply for Grant of Probate or Grant of Administration;
OR
if the applicant is entitled to the entire estate if the amount is less than $75,000 (depending on relationship of the beneficiary to the deceased; see
https://www.unclaimedproperties.bankofcanada.ca/
for details), then proceed as follows.
When the applicant first arrives at the credit union
1.
Provide the Estate Transfer with No Will Form (ETNW 2351). Advise the member to complete the form as instructed in the guidelines on the form. If there is insufficient space on the form to record the bonds, advise the member to attach and initial a separate list.
It is not necessary to have this form notarized if it is
signed by an authorized credit union signing officer; and
stamped with the credit union芒聙聶s Endorsement Stamp.
Any changes or deletions must be stamped 芒聙聹Alterations and Erasure Guaranteed芒聙聺 and signed by both the Declarant and, under the credit union name, a credit union signing officer.
2.
Notify applicant(s) that when the completed Estate Transfer with No Will Form (ETNW 2351) is returned to the credit union, it must be accompanied by the following:
a Notarial Copy of the Proof of Death (see Chapter 4, Government Bonds), and
all bonds to be transferred.
When the beneficiary returns documents to the credit union
1.
Verify that the Estate Transfer with No Will Form (ENTW 2351) is completed correctly, signed by an authorized credit union signing officer, stamped with the credit union芒聙聶s Endorsement Stamp, and accompanied by the Proof of Death and bonds.
If there are persons other than the applicant entitled to a portion of the estate, ensure that Sections C and D of the Estate Transfer with No Will Form (ETNW 2351) have been completed. The submission of an unprobated Will does not reduce the requirements relating to the law of intestacy. If there are no other heirs or next-of-kin, the word 芒聙聹NIL芒聙聺 is entered in the Name of Beneficiary field.
Note:	A minor who is entitled to inherit should be listed in Section C, with age and relationship to the deceased specified.
If the name of the deceased varies between the Registered Owner's name on the bond(s) and the name on the Proof of Death, the name specified on the Estate Transfer with No Will Form (ENTW 2351) for deceased should include name variations as AKA (i.e., also known as). If not, a Letter of Name Guarantee from the credit union is required.
2.
If required, complete and guarantee a Letter of Name Guarantee 芒聙聯 Estate, Form 1350, for the deceased and/or the applicant where
the name of the applicant(s) on the Estate Transfer with No Will Form (ETNW 2351) is not exactly as the name to be registered on the transferred bonds; or
the name of the deceased varies and is not entered on the Estate Transfer with No Will Form (ETNW 2351) as AKA.
3.
Complete a Notification of Bond Transfer, Form 1324, in duplicate. Retain a copy for credit union files.
4.
F
orward the bond(s), Proof of Death, and the Estate Transfer with No Will Form (ETNW 2351), a copy of Notification of Bond Transfer, and (if required) a Letter of Name Guarantee to Central 1, as per internal credit union transportation policy by courier, at the following address:
Central 1 Credit Union
Payment Services
Attention: Tracing
1441 Creekside Drive
Vancouver, BC V6J 4S7
Related Information
Notification of Bond Transfer,
Form 1324
Letter of Name Guarantee 芒聙聯 Estate,
Form 1350
Deceased Died Intestate (No Will) 芒聙聯 Grant of Administration Obtained
If the Registered Owner dies Intestate (without a Will) and a Grant of Administration has been granted by the court, proceed as follows.
1.
Obtain a notarial or court-certified copy of the Grant of Administration.
Note:	A Proof of Death certificate is not required.
2.
Complete and guarantee the Estate Transfer with No Will Form (ETNW 2351) as instructed in the guidelines on the form.
3.
Any changes to the Estate Transfer with No Will Form (ETNW 2351) must be stamped 芒聙聹Alterations and Erasure Guaranteed芒聙聺 and signed by both the Declarant and, under the credit union name, a credit union signing officer.
4.
Guarantee the transferor芒聙聶s signature on the Estate Transfer with No Will Form (ETNW 2351). Stamp with the credit union芒聙聶s Endorsement Stamp and have an authorized signing officer on file with Central 1 sign under the stamp.
5.
If required, complete a Letter of Name Guarantee 芒聙聯 Estate, Form 1350, for the Declarant.
6.
Complete a Notification of Bond Transfer, Form 1324, in duplicate. Retain a copy for the credit union芒聙聶s files.
7.
Forward the bond(s), Grant of Administration, the Estate Transfer with No Will Form (ETNW 2351), a copy of Notification of Bond Transfer, and (if necessary) a Letter of Name Guarantee to Central 1, as per internal credit union transportation policy by courier, to the following address:
Central 1 Credit Union
Payment Services
Attention: Tracing
1441 Creekside Drive
Vancouver, BC V6J 4S7
Related Information
Notification of Bond Transfer,
Form 1324
Letter of Name Guarantee 芒聙聯 Estate,
Form 1350
11.4	Transferring Bonds to a Trust (Registered Plan)
Beneficiaries or surviving registrants may opt to transfer the bonds of a deceased into a trust governing one of the following:
Registered Retirement Savings Plan
Registered Retirement Income Fund
Registered Pension Plan
Deferred Profit Sharing Plan
Employee Profit Sharing Plan
To transfer bonds to a registered plan, proceed as follows.
1.
Complete Transfer of Ownership Form (TRF 533). Include the following information:
Trust to which the bonds are being transferred and amount being transferred
Address of trust
Social insurance number of each participant in trust
2.
Guarantee the transferor芒聙聶s signature on the Transfer of Ownership Form (TRF 533). Stamp the form with the credit union芒聙聶s Endorsement Stamp and have an authorized signing officer on file with Central 1 sign under the stamp.
11.5	Transferring Bonds Registered to a Deceased Resident of the United States
The following documents are required for bonds registered to a resident of the United States at the time of death:
Notarial Copy of document equivalent to Grant of Probate or Grant of Administration
Estate Transfer with Will Form (ETW 2351)
Complete and guarantee the Estate Transfer with Will Form (ETW 2351) as instructed in Section 11.3, Subsection, Deceased Died Testate (with Will) 芒聙聯 Grant of Probate Obtained. The executor or administrator of the deceased's estate signs as transferor.
END
